title: Agent Olympics Live Runner Config
description: Operator-supplied per-round runner configuration for scripts/live-runner.js. Maps round-manifest participants to dispatch transports. Credentials are referenced by class/handle only — credential values must never appear in this file.

description.description: Optional operator notes about this configuration.

round_manifest.description: Default round manifest path this config was written for (informational; the CLI argument is authoritative).

run_directory.description: Override for the round manifest's run_directory. Recommended so live-runner output never collides with round.js/ci-round run directories.

tasks.description: Optional task_id filter. When present, only these tasks from the round manifest are dispatched.

participant_id.description: Must match an agent_id in the round manifest's participants list.

adapter.description: Adapter/runtime label recorded in the dispatch record (e.g. hermes, openclaw, stub, cli).

transport.description: Dispatch transport. Only local_exec (argv spawn, no shell) is implemented in this repository. Network transports (A2A, ssh brokers) are operator extension points configured as local_exec argv commands.

execution_profile.description: dry_run: no credentials are made available (credential_class recorded as none). live: requires credential references, operator approval, and a passing readiness declaration.

command.description: Dispatch command as an argv array (spawned directly — never through a shell). Placeholders: {envelope} {run_dir} {agent_id} {run_id} {task_id} {round_id} {time_limit_minutes} {seed}.

time_limit_ms_override.description: Operator/test override of the enforced wall-clock limit in milliseconds. When absent the envelope/task time_limit_minutes is enforced. The override is recorded in the dispatch record.

credential_class.description: Class of credential made available (e.g. gateway_session). Use 'none' for dry_run.

handling.description: live profiles must use reference_only — the runner never reads or stores credential values.

ref.description: Value-free reference/path/secret-provider handle (e.g. secret-provider://...). Never a credential value.

participant_may_read.description: Whether the participant process is allowed to read the referenced credential.

approver.description: Who approved this credential-bearing run (operator handle).

approval_ref.description: Reference to the approval record (issue/PR/doc link).

readiness_declaration.description: Path to a live-runner-readiness input fixture. Required for live profiles; must evaluate to decision 'ready' via scripts/live-runner-readiness.js.
